comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
I reviewed solution.tex against the three tasks and verified its mathematical core numerically, then closed the one genuine rigor gap.

**Verification (run_python).**
- Spearman–Brown ICC link: ICC(2)=k·ICC(1)/(1+(k−1)ICC(1)) reproduced *exactly* in a balanced random-effects simulation; ICC(1) converges to σ²_b/(σ²_b+σ²_w) as claimed (Prop. 2/sb).
- Disattenuation identity φ = ρ_obs/√(ρ_PS ρ_c'): simulated observed r=0.448 with reliabilities 0.7,0.8 → disattenuated 0.599 vs true φ=0.6 (Lemma disatt) ✓.
- α=ω under tau-equivalence: both equal 0.88479 to machine precision (Prop. alpha) ✓.

**Gap fixed.** Theorem (validity of the χ²-difference test), part (i), previously hand-waved the regularity of the LR test at φ=1. This is a real subtlety: for a *correlation* parameter, φ=1 is on the boundary |φ|≤1, where Wilks' theorem can yield a Chernoff/Self–Liang mixture rather than a clean χ²₁. I rewrote (i) to:
  (a) reparametrise the merged-vs-two-factor comparison so the null "φ=1" becomes "δ=0" (the second factor's deviation-from-proportionality loadings vanish), an *interior* point with nonsingular Fisher information, restoring the genuine χ²₁ limit by classical Wilks; and
  (b) note that if one insists on the raw one-sided correlation null, the conservative ½χ²₀+½χ²₁ mixture applies, which only lowers the p-value — so the T>3.84 rejection rule remains valid (indeed conservative). 

This makes the discriminant-validity test honestly justified at the boundary, which previously was the weakest link.

**Status.** The solution is otherwise complete and rigorous: it correctly separates unconditional mathematical content (conceptual distinctness; validity/identifiability/sizing of every inferential criterion) from explicitly flagged empirical inputs (EI1–EI4), and proves the conditional verdict (Theorem verdict). LaTeX toolchain is not installed in this environment so compilation was skipped, but the source is well-formed (amsmath provides smallmatrix). Resolving.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
Independent re-verification (second solver pass). Issue is already resolved; I re-checked the solution and confirm it stands.

Numerical re-checks (run_python):
- Spearman--Brown ICC link from a balanced one-way random-effects simulation: ICC(2) computed directly from MSB/MSW equals k*ICC(1)/(1+(k-1)ICC(1)) to all printed digits (0.762909 = 0.762909); ICC(1) tracks the population fraction sigma_b^2/(sigma_b^2+sigma_w^2) (0.39 vs 0.43 at J=400). [Prop. sb]
- Disattenuation: r_obs = phi*sqrt(rho_PS*rho_c) inverts to phi exactly (0.6). [Lemma disatt]
- alpha = omega under tau-equivalence: both 0.884793 to machine precision. [Prop. alpha]

Source audit: the boundary-regularity fix for Theorem (validity of the chi^2-difference test) part (i) is present (Chernoff/Self--Liang caveat, delta=0 interior reparametrisation restoring the clean chi^2_1 limit, and the conservative 1/2 chi^2_0 + 1/2 chi^2_1 fallback that keeps the T>3.84 rule valid). Nesting (df diff = 1), three-indicator identifiability, FWL-based incremental-validity identification, and the explicit EI1-EI4/conditional-verdict separation are all in place and rigorous.

No further mathematical gap found. (LaTeX toolchain unavailable here so compilation not run, but source is well-formed.) Leaving status resolved.
